_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for every quantitative claim in draft_paper_0411_v3.txt and sn-article_0411_v3.tex. Every number here is traceable to a file under code/0411/results/. No value in this file may be fabricated, rounded beyond its source precision, or re-derived without updating the source_file entry.

accuracy_fixed_eval.note: All five surrogates achieve stress R2 in 0.924-0.929 and keff R2 in 0.866-0.877. data-mono-ineq is the primary model on the strength of the tightest stress RMSE and PICP within the target 90-95% band; differences across models are modest and should not be overclaimed.

main_text_mu_only_at_sigma_k_1.explanation: y = mu(theta), epistemic-only propagation through the heteroscedastic surrogate. Used as the main-text canonical because it isolates parametric uncertainty from surrogate aleatoric noise.

appendix_predictive_at_sigma_k_1.explanation: y = mu(theta) + sigma(theta) * eps, epistemic + aleatoric draw. Reported in the appendix as a sanity check; predictive values inflate stress std from 30.47 to 32.15 MPa and P(sigma>131) from 0.8531 to 0.8363.

iter1_keff_methodological_note.fact: iter1_keff in dataset_v3.csv has standard deviation ~5e-14 across all 3124 rows (numerical noise floor).
iter1_keff_methodological_note.interpretation: Under the current LHS design, iteration-1 reactivity is evaluated at the reference geometry and temperature, where the 8 sampled material parameters (E, nu, alpha, SS316 thermal properties) do not enter the neutron cross-section tables. iter1_keff is therefore effectively constant under parametric perturbation and carries no propagated uncertainty of its own. Only the coupled iter2_keff, which responds to thermo-mechanical feedback, has meaningful parametric variability.
iter1_keff_methodological_note.manuscript_wording: Keep this as 1-2 sentences of methodological justification only. DO NOT quote any numerical compression ratio (such as '6300 pcm -> 66 pcm' or '95-fold'); the 6300 pcm figure has no data support and has been removed from the manuscript.

iteration2_max_global_stress.interpretation: Stress is dominated by E_intercept (S1~0.59, CI excludes zero) and secondarily by alpha_base (S1~0.17). Per CLAUDE.md rule, SS316_alpha's S1 CI crosses zero and must not be written as stable dominant factor.

iteration2_keff.note: Memory project_sobol_canonical.md records alpha_base S1~0.775 as canonical for keff; values are pulled from 0411/results/sobol/data-mono-ineq/sobol_full.json at manuscript build time.

posterior.noise_model: true HF output + 2 percent artificial Gaussian noise (simulated measurement)
posterior.note_on_accept_rate: data-mono-ineq and phy-mono accept rates are lower because their MCMC proposal_scale was retuned to 0.40 (see commit b1139ea); this is intentional and the chains remain well mixed.

feasible_region_summary.interpretation: Across the 10 high-stress feasibility-check cases, none pass the 90% feasibility gate at tau=131 MPa; 4 of 10 have posterior P(stress>131) essentially equal to 1.0, confirming the gate behaves correctly on high-risk parameter points.

hf_baseline.teardown_note: returncode = 1 for all 3 HF runs due to a known MPI_FINALIZE teardown bug; the scientific computation completes and writes all expected outputs. 'use time' (printed by the HF driver on its last stdout line) is the authoritative baseline; wall clock is reported for reference.

manuscript_wording_rules: Quote HF baseline as 'approximately 2266 s (about 38 min)' in methods and discussion.
Do NOT use the legacy 3600 s figure anywhere; it was a hand-written placeholder in paper_speedup_benchmark.json and has been retired.
Do NOT use the legacy 2000 s figure either; it was a rounding artefact from before the detailed benchmark was parsed.

hf_rerun_direct_validation.manuscript_wording: Present as a direct HF spot-check: 'Five independent HF evaluations, including three strictly new LHS draws and one HF refill of a dataset row whose iter2 outputs were originally NaN, all fall within one predicted standard deviation; stress MAE is 0.7 MPa on this validation set.' Do NOT promote to 'large-scale generalization' or 'comprehensive external test'. The nearest-neighbour retrieval baseline has been REMOVED from the manuscript per user directive; it is retained in the archive only for traceability.

retired_numbers.description: Figures from earlier drafts that have NO data support and MUST NOT appear in draft_paper_0411_v3.txt or sn-article_0411_v3.tex.
